rationale: The citing paper uses Deleersnyder et al. (2009) repeatedly to frame core prior evidence on business-cycle-related advertising behavior, including procyclical ad spending and its weaker long-run financial outcomes. It is cited in the literature review and later discussion as a key benchmark for what was previously known at aggregate or firm level. The authors then position their contribution as addressing those limits by estimating brand-level short- and long-term price and advertising elasticities over expansions and contractions.
relation_description: The citing study extends Deleersnyder et al. (2009) by moving from prior aggregate/firm-level advertising-cycle evidence to a more granular brand-level analysis and by adding price effectiveness alongside advertising. It keeps the same broad business-cycle marketing question but broadens scope and detail of the response analysis.
